The real Florida table

Stone crab.
Grouper. Swordfish.
The whole catch.

When local boats bring it in, it lands on your plate. Stone Crab Claws, Grouper, Soft Shell Crab, Swordfish — the menu follows the water, not a schedule.

Prime Rib carved nightly. Everything made here. Nothing shipped from somewhere else.

Stone Crab Claws Fresh Grouper Swordfish Soft Shell Crab Prime Rib Nightly
See the Full Menu
From the kitchen

Stars of
the season

Seasonal — Limited Daily

Stone Crab Claws

Pulled from local Florida waters, served cold with mustard sauce. Order early — they go fast once season opens in October.

Chef’s Daily Selection

Fresh Catch

Grouper, Swordfish, Soft Shell Crab — whatever the boats brought in. Grilled, blackened, or sauced your way.

Nightly Feature

Prime Rib

Slow-roasted and hand-carved every evening. The landlubber’s answer at a seafood house — always on our specials.

The atmosphere

Waterfront.
Live music.
Old Florida soul.

Dine on the screened lanai, the waterside patio, or inside the cozy bar. The best live entertainment in Collier County plays while the dock lights shimmer at dusk. This is what Florida felt like before the condos arrived.

Since 1978

Part of
Goodland’s
soul.

Little Bar isn’t just a restaurant. For 46 years it’s been the heartbeat of this fishing village — the place locals bring their families, and where strangers become regulars by their second visit.

Stone Crab season brings the faithful back. The dock lights shimmer. The band plays. Same as it ever was.
